name: Shanghai
lifestyle: Cosmopolitan financial capital with premium expat enclaves in the Former French Concession and Jing'an. International school tuition is typically the largest line item for families, and imported goods carry a steep premium. Z visa sponsorship is the standard path; direct investor routes are limited.

true_fat_fire.description: Former French Concession lane house or Lujiazui penthouse with live-in ayi plus driver. Private-kitchen dinners weekly, wine club, 3+ international trips annually.

guide.groceries: Hema, imported grocers, and premium online delivery baskets with strong seafood, fruit, wine, and imported pantry coverage.
guide.dining: Fine dining, hotel bars, and chef-led tasting menus show up regularly, with nicer nights often around RMB800-2,000 per person.
guide.rhythm: Driver and ayi support, high-touch delivery culture, premium gyms or wellness, and a social calendar centered on restaurants, bars, and luxury retail.

comfortable_expat.description: Jing'an/Xintiandi/FFC 2BR serviced apartment. Dining out 3-4 nights/week mixing Chinese and Western, part-time ayi, regional travel plus one long-haul trip.

guide.groceries: A mix of Hema, Sam's-style bulk restocks, and imported shelves for cheese, coffee, wine, and better proteins without going fully elite.
guide.dining: Comfortable restaurant spending 2-3 times a week plus a nicer dinner every month or so, often RMB120-250 casually and RMB600-1,200 when stepping up.
guide.rhythm: Mostly metro plus rideshare, delivery-heavy convenience, regular salon or wellness spend, and short regional trips layered into city life.

luxury_family.description: Changning/Minhang 3-4BR compound house near SAS or BISS, full-time ayi plus driver, top international school access when dependents apply, annual ski/beach plus summer home-country trip.

guide.groceries: Big family provisioning through imported grocers, Hema, and bulk delivery: school snacks, fruit, proteins, and household staples in volume.
guide.dining: Family-friendly mall or hotel dining most weekends plus adult meals out, with casual family meals often RMB150-300 per person and destination dinners much higher.
guide.rhythm: Ayi and driver support, premium kid logistics, clubs or tutoring, and an annual calendar built around school breaks and long-haul travel.
